associativeOnly: Report endpoint effects as pre-specified analyses only; deviations are post-hoc
Safety claims are descriptive; do not make regulatory or actionability claims
requiresEvidence: Effect sizes (HR, OR, RR, mean difference) must cite the SAP-named primary/secondary table and its CI
Population denominators must cite the ADSL-derived analysis-set summary

evidenceRules.description: Plan §S5.57 — every claim about a clinical endpoint must be accompanied by a subgroup_summary.json that breaks the analysis-set sample count by ethnicity, sex, age, and any pre-specified additional axes. Missing breakdown blocks the claim. Required columns: subgroup, n, ethnicity, sex, age_band. Minimum n per subgroup: 5. Variance thresholds must be pre-specified.

Plan §S5.57 — agent must emit consort_ai_checklist.json with all 14 CONSORT-AI 2020 extension items addressed (AI-system identity, intended-use population, training-data lineage, performance bounds, fairness audit, blinding, statistical methods, demographics, error-handling, outcomes, harms, generalisability, interpretation, funding). 100% completion required. Closes the CONSORT 2025 gap flagged in Lancet 2025.

note: Clinical-trial overlay: SAP-style entity patterns, endpoint/arm/subgroup vocabulary, hazard/odds/risk effect-size columns. Plan §S5.57 — subgroup-fairness rule + CONSORT-AI 14-item checklist (Round-4 §22.18 PCCP-friendly posture; closes Lancet 2025 CONSORT gap). Merged on top of interpretation-policy.json at load time; overlay values replace base values field-by-field.
